# UC-11.6.4 · Proofpoint Spam and Content Filter False-Positive Rate Trending

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** IT Operations &middot; **Type:** Quality, Operations &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We watch how Proofpoint treats and rewrites mail, so threats and DLP events line up with what the security team already sees in their console.*

---

## Description

Aggressive anti-spam settings reduce malware but starve the business of legitimate mail. Trending false-positive or release overrides from filter logs quantifies user pain and guides policy easing before executives miss critical messages.

## Value

Balances deliverability with security, reduces help-desk volume, and documents messaging quality for customer-facing teams.

## Implementation

(1) Ingest filter verdict and release telemetry; field names vary by POD—validate extractions. (2) Join to service-desk tickets tagged email for ground truth. (3) Alert when FP rate exceeds baseline. (4) Segment bulk vs strict spam. (5) Coordinate changes with messaging admins.

## SPL

```spl
index=email sourcetype="proofpoint:filter" earliest=-30d@d
| eval verdict=coalesce(verdict, Verdict, disposition, Disposition, "")
| eval fp_flag=coalesce(false_positive, user_release, admin_override, "")
| bin _time span=1d
| stats count(eval(match(lower(verdict),"(?i)spam|bulk|block"))) as filtered count(eval(match(lower(fp_flag),"(?i)yes|true|released|override"))) as fp_signals count as total by _time
| eval fp_rate_pct=round(100*fp_signals/total,3)
| timechart span=1d avg(fp_rate_pct) as avg_fp_rate
```

## Visualization

Line chart (false-positive rate over time), bar chart (releases by rule), table of top affected recipients.

## Known False Positives

Aggressive content rules during phishing waves; more releases and overrides are expected in those weeks.

## References

- [Proofpoint support — message filtering](https://help.proofpoint.com/)
